name: Chef Tariq Al Mansoori
bio: Tariq forages desert botanicals and reimagines Gulf flavors with modern precision. Date syrup becomes a glossy gastrique; dried limes star in umami broths; saffron rice shows up as bite-sized arancini with ouzi lamb filling. He roasts over date-palm charcoal and serves Arabic Qahwa like a ritual. Sunrise dune drives reset his menu brainstorms, and yes, his spice tins are labeled in Arabic calligraphy. Tariq’s dinners are warm, generous, and deeply rooted.
eventDescription: Desert Bloom tells a calm, generous story of the dunes. Date-palm charcoal and dried lime set the tone while saffron warmth ties the room together. You release the scent of loomi between your palms, sip Qahwa, and taste a broth that feels sun-cured and steady. Colors move from sand gold to night rose. It is hospitality first and precision right alongside it.
